methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Pipedream is a Series A transportation company founded by Garrett McCurrach and Canon Reeves that has raised $22.4M to date. The transportation sector comp universe includes 8 private peers (REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and others) and no public comparables, limiting traditional peer benchmarking approaches.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable valuation method. Using total raised ($22.4M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $44.8M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while five transportation peers exist broadly, the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-tier) yielded fewer than three datapoints, making sector-median comparison unreliable and potentially misleading.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $38M (low) to $52M (high), with a median of $45M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single method. Key uncertainties include the validity of the 2.0x stage-progression multiplier for this specific company and subsector dynamics within transportation, where peer valuations span wide ranges. A more robust analysis would require either (1) access to adjacent-stage transportation comparables or (2) revenue or operational metrics to ground an earnings or revenue multiple approach.
